action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 20 achieved a new best test AP of 0.2495 by ensembling TTA predictions from both best_state_dict and ema_state_dict (16 runs each, 32 total). Remaining time is ~31 minutes — too short for another full training run, but enough for additional low-risk inference optimization. Analysis: TTA worked well (+0.0016 over single-model TTA). Increasing the number of stochastic forward passes per model from 16 to 32 should further reduce prediction variance and may squeeze out additional AP. To keep wall-clock time reasonable, I will also raise the inference batch size from 512 to 1024, which easily fits in 31 GB VRAM. Decision: Update predict.py with batch_size=1024 and tta_runs_per_model=32, then immediately submit eval on the existing checkpoints/best.pt. If this succeeds with time to spare, I will attempt a short fine-tuning run next.
